title: Bare local node server script is REVIEW — unverified local source (ADR 0011 Direction 2, RC-OBS-02)

origin.description: Shape-preserving redaction of a real committed claude_desktop_config.json from the public repo JacquesGariepy/game-assistant-mcp (RC-B06, 0.3.0-rc.0 window). The repo carries no detectable license, so only the non-copyrightable config shape is retained: a single server launched via a local 'node <path>/server.js' command with no env and no args beyond the script path. The identifying Windows username was neutralized.
redactions: license omitted: source repo has no detectable license; stored as a shape-preserving redacted snapshot, not redistributed verbatim

humanExpectation.rationale: A bare local 'node <script>.js' command runs a local script CallLint never inspects. Its source is observable (the command string is right there) but not independently verifiable — it is not a recognized package or a pinned image. Per ADR 0011 Direction 2 (accepted), exec.unverified-local-source fires (REVIEW): SAFE is reachable only for recognized, inspectable sources, so this asks a human to confirm the local source rather than asserting it is dangerous.
knownLimitations: RC-OBS-02: the finding is 'we did not inspect this local source', not 'this source is malicious'. A developer running their own server.js is normal; the REVIEW is a confirmation prompt, carried by the finding's falsePositiveNote. It is NOT a dangerous false-SAFE either way — the source was never hidden (contrast C031, where an unrecognized shape hid the source and resolves to UNKNOWN).
